title: If You Build It, They Will Come
content_html:
Several times in my life I’ve tried to break in with a new social group. I ran into a cool community I wanted to join, or I moved to a new city and wanted to make friends there, or I just wanted to broaden my horizons.

Pretty quickly I learned that the best and fastest way to join a group is to organize events where we do the group’s core activity. In every group I’ve ever encountered, there is far more demand for social events and things to do than there is supply. Getting people to come is like giving away ice cream at the beach. You can make friends by showing up from the outside and consistently attending other people’s events, but it’s a lot easier and faster to make friends if you’re also organizing your own stuff and inviting the people you’re hoping to get closer to, or helping with someone else’s events. I’ve found this true everywhere from niche online fanfiction discussion groups to vanguard intellectual movements directing hundreds of millions of dollars annually.

Lots of people have a sort of consumer attitude towards their communities, where they take everything for granted. I saw things this way when I was young. A social scene is an automatic feature of the world that appears on its own, like a wild blueberry bush. It starts sprouting parties and dinners and conferences and reading groups as naturally as the bush sprouts berries.

Surprisingly, it turns out things don’t actually work that way. In fact, events happen when someone puts in the legwork to organize them. And one of the most reliable laws of the universe is that, if something takes a little bit of legwork, then most people just won’t do it. A scene’s leaders are mostly the people who actually bother to put in the work.

The work of organizing is underappreciated by many people. But the other organizers are very attuned to this, and absolutely will notice who else is picking up the burdens.

I’ve come to believe that part of today’s problem of social alienation is a problem of too many free riders. Lots of people want to consume social fabric, but our social scripts telling people to produce social fabric have largely fallen by the wayside. I don’t know how to solve this undersupply at the scale of society. But you can solve it at the scale of your own community by just supplying it.

sentences.en: In every group I’ve ever encountered, there is far more demand for social events and things to do than there is supply.
sentences.zh: 在我遇到的每个群体中，对社交活动和活动的需求远远超过供应。

sentences.en: Getting people to come is like giving away ice cream at the beach.
sentences.zh: 让人们来就像在海滩上赠送冰淇淋一样。

sentences.en: You can make friends by showing up from the outside and consistently attending other people’s events, but it’s a lot easier and faster to make friends if you’re also organizing your own stuff and inviting the people you’re hoping to get closer to, or helping with someone else’s events.
sentences.zh: 您可以通过从外部露面并持续参加其他人的活动来交朋友，但如果您还整理自己的东西并邀请您希望亲近的人，或者帮助别人的活动，那么交朋友会更容易、更快。

sentences.en: I’ve found this true everywhere from niche online fanfiction discussion groups to vanguard intellectual movements directing hundreds of millions of dollars annually.
sentences.zh: 我发现从小众在线同人小说讨论小组到每年引导数亿美元的先锋知识分子运动，到处都是如此。

sentences.en: I saw things this way when I was young.
sentences.zh: 我年轻时就是这样看事情的。

sentences.en: A social scene is an automatic feature of the world that appears on its own, like a wild blueberry bush.
sentences.zh: 社交场景是世界自动出现的特征，就像野生蓝莓丛一样。

sentences.en: It starts sprouting parties and dinners and conferences and reading groups as naturally as the bush sprouts berries.
sentences.zh: 它开始萌芽聚会、晚宴、会议和阅读小组，就像灌木丛发芽浆果一样自然。

And one of the most reliable laws of the universe is that, if something takes a little bit of legwork, then most people just won’t do it. A scene’s leaders are mostly the people who actually bother to put in the work.

sentences.en: Surprisingly, it turns out things don’t actually work that way.
sentences.zh: 令人惊讶的是，事实证明事情实际上并非如此。

sentences.en: In fact, events happen when someone puts in the legwork to organize them.
sentences.zh: 事实上，当有人付出努力来组织事件时，事件就会发生。

sentences.en: And one of the most reliable laws of the universe is that, if something takes a little bit of legwork, then most people just won’t do it.
sentences.zh: 宇宙中最可靠的法则之一是，如果某件事需要一点跑腿的功夫，那么大多数人就不会去做。

sentences.en: A scene’s leaders are mostly the people who actually bother to put in the work.
sentences.zh: 一个场景的领导者大多是那些真正愿意投入工作的人。

sentences.en: The work of organizing is underappreciated by many people.
sentences.zh: 许多人低估了组织工作。

sentences.en: But the other organizers are very attuned to this, and absolutely will notice who else is picking up the burdens.
sentences.zh: 但其他组织者对此非常适应，并且绝对会注意到还有谁在承担责任。

blocks.html_en: I’ve come to believe that part of today’s problem of social alienation is a problem of too many free riders. Lots of people want to consume social fabric, but our social scripts telling people to produce social fabric have largely fallen by the wayside. I don’t know how to solve this undersupply at the scale of society. But you can solve it at the scale of your own community by just supplying it.

sentences.en: I’ve come to believe that part of today’s problem of social alienation is a problem of too many free riders.
sentences.zh: 我开始相信，当今社会疏远问题的部分原因是搭便车人数过多。

sentences.en: Lots of people want to consume social fabric, but our social scripts telling people to produce social fabric have largely fallen by the wayside.
sentences.zh: 很多人想要消费社会结构，但我们告诉人们生产社会结构的社会脚本基本上已经被抛在了一边。

sentences.en: I don’t know how to solve this undersupply at the scale of society.
sentences.zh: 我不知道如何解决社会层面的供给不足问题。

sentences.en: But you can solve it at the scale of your own community by just supplying it.
sentences.zh: 但你只需提供它就可以在你自己社区的规模上解决这个问题。

content_html_zh:
在我的一生中，我曾多次尝试融入一个新的社交群体。 我遇到了一个我想加入的很酷的社区，或者我搬到了一个新城市并想在那里交朋友，或者我只是想拓宽我的视野。

很快我就了解到加入一个团体的最好、最快的方式是组织活动，让我们进行团体的核心活动。 在我遇到的每个群体中，对社交活动和活动的需求远远超过供应。 让人们来就像在海滩上赠送冰淇淋一样。 您可以通过从外部露面并持续参加其他人的活动来交朋友，但如果您还整理自己的东西并邀请您希望亲近的人，或者帮助别人的活动，那么交朋友会更容易、更快。 我发现从小众在线同人小说讨论小组到每年引导数亿美元的先锋知识分子运动，到处都是如此。

许多人对他们的社区抱有一种消费态度，他们认为一切都是理所当然的。 我年轻时就是这样看事情的。 社交场景是世界自动出现的特征，就像野生蓝莓丛一样。 它开始萌芽聚会、晚宴、会议和阅读小组，就像灌木丛发芽浆果一样自然。

令人惊讶的是，事实证明事情实际上并非如此。 事实上，当有人付出努力来组织事件时，事件就会发生。 宇宙中最可靠的法则之一是，如果某件事需要一点跑腿的功夫，那么大多数人就不会去做。 一个场景的领导者大多是那些真正愿意投入工作的人。

许多人低估了组织工作。 但其他组织者对此非常适应，并且绝对会注意到还有谁在承担责任。

我开始相信，当今社会疏远问题的部分原因是搭便车人数过多。 很多人想要消费社会结构，但我们告诉人们生产社会结构的社会脚本基本上已经被抛在了一边。 我不知道如何解决社会层面的供给不足问题。 但你只需提供它就可以在你自己社区的规模上解决这个问题。
